rulings.Case Title: CHASE MANHATTAN BANK, USA, N.A., A CORP. VS. EMIL A YUSUPOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Order Permitting Late Filing Of Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 16, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT EMIL YUSUPOV'S Motion For Court Order Permitting Late Filing Of Claim Of Exemption IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED IF MOVING PARTY PROVIDES PROOF OF SERVICE OF MOTION AND ORDER SHORTENING TIME. IF PROOF OF SERVICE NOT PROVIDED, MOTION WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS GRAVELY VS. A.W. CHESTERON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens; Or In The Alternative, To Transfer Venue To San Diego County
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 16, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANT WEYERHAEUSER COMPANY'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens; Or In The Alternative, To Transfer Venue To San Diego County: THE MOTION TO TRANSFER TO OREGON IS GRANTED. WEYERHAEUSER HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT ALL BUT ONE DEFENDANT IS SUBJECT TO SERVICE OF PROCESS IN OREGON AND THAT THE OREGON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DOES NOT RUN UNTIL MARCH 2008. PLAINTIFF'S WORK EXPERIENCE IN CALIFORNIA (BASED ON HIS SWORN TESTIMONY) IS LIMITED AND HIS MEDICAL TREATMENT HAS NOT OCCURED IN CALIFORNIA. AS A LONG TIME RESIDENT OF OREGON, WHERE HE SPENT MOST OF HIS WORKING CAREER, IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD BE PREJUDICED IN ANYWAY BY TRANSFERRING THE CASE TO OREGON. THE UNSUPPORTED DECLARATION OF KELLER IS NOT A BASIS TO DENY THE MOTION. =(302/PJM/CB)

rulings.Case Title: MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION VS. THE BOMBAY COMPANY INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Plaintiff Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement As To Defendant Sur La Table Inc.

rulings.Case Title: FAISAL HABEED VS. FRANK BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-07-621703 WITH CASE CGC-07-462076
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 16, 2007, LINE 5. PLAINTIFF FAISAL HABEED'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-07-621703 WITH CASE CGC-07-462076: HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS MOOTNESS AND LANDLORD'S STANDING TO OPPOSE MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE/STAY. ASSUMING THAT LANDLORD HAS STANDING, WHY WOULD THE CONSOLIDATION OF THE DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION DELAY RESOLUTION OF THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION? =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint And First Amended Answer After Reclassification As An Ordinary Civil Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 16, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANTS LENT HOWARD AND CHRISTOPHER DOLAN'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint And First Amended Answer After Reclassification As An Ordinary Civil Action: CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS REQUESTED LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED ANSWER AND CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE REQUEST IS GRANTED AS TO THE ANSWER AND PLAINTIFF MAY CHALLENGE THE AMENDMENT BY DEMURRER. THE REQUEST IS DENIED AS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT BECAUSE THE PROPOSED PLEADING FAILS TO CONFORM TO NOTICE PLEADING AND IS REPLETE WITH UNNECESSARY EVIDENTIARY ALLEGATIONS ALLEGEDLY DESIGNED TO HARASS PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/KD)
